name: AAA Old Delete - NeoIPC Admission birth weight
shortName: AAA Old Delete - NeoIPC Adm. BW

name: NeoIPC Admission on day of life
description: For infants that have not been delivered in your own hospital, record the infant's day of life on the day of admission (day of birth = day of life 1. The next day, starting at 00:00, is the second day of life.)

name: NeoIPC Admission Length of Stay
description: Length of Stay (should always be 0). For Reporting purpose only

name: NeoIPC Admission type
description: Describes if the infant was born in your hospital or if it was admitted after birth and if so, how long after birth.

name: NeoIPC BSI Antibiotic treatment
formName: Intravenous antibiotic therapy for five or more days initiated
description: Antibiotic treatment for at least five days was initiated. The day of the first dose and the day of the last dose are counted. Days where no dose was administered between the first and the last dose (e.g., skipped doses because of high drug levels in therapeutic drug monitoring) are counted as if a dose had been administered. Days after the last dose are not counted regardless of the patient's measured or assumed drug level. If the infant died, was discharged, or transferred before the end of the five-day course of intravenous antibiotics, this condition is met if treatment was scheduled for five days or more.

name: NeoIPC BSI Acidosis
description: Unexplained metabolic acidosis with a base deficit greater than 10 mmol/L (base excess more negative than -10 mmol/L).

name: NeoIPC BSI Apnoea
formName: New/more frequent episodes of apnea or increases in oxygen demand or ventilatory support
description: New or increased frequency of episodes of apnea lasting more than 20 seconds, or an increase in the amount of oxygen required to maintain adequate oxygenation, or an escalation of ventilatory support, such as increased flow with high-flow therapy or intubation for invasive ventilation.

name: NeoIPC BSI Bradycardias
description: New/more frequent episodes of bradycardia with a heart rate of less than 80 beats per minute or otherwise unexplained tachycardia with a resting heart rate of more than 200 beats per minute.

name: NeoIPC BSI CRP
description: There is at least one laboratory test result with a C-reactive protein (CRP) value of more than 10 mg/L (1 mg/dL).

name: NeoIPC BSI Device association
description: An infection is considered as vascular catheter-associated if the catheter has been present for at least three consecutive days on the day of infection (=first symptoms or first positive diagnostic test; e.g. culture) or the day before. Both, central venous catheter (CVC) association and peripheral venous catheter (PVC) association are recorded. If a patient developing a bloodstream infection meets the criteria for both, PVC- and CVC-association, the CVC is considered as the device with the relatively higher infection risk and the BSI should be recorded as CVC-associated BSI.

name: NeoIPC BSI Day of life
description: The day of life on which the bloodstream infection occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

name: NeoIPC BSI Feeding intolerance
formName: Enteral feeding intolerance, abdominal distension or ileus
description: Enteral feeding intolerance, abdominal distension or ileus without imaging findings or surgical findings that would otherwise suggest a necrotizing entrocolitis or a spontaneous intestinal perforation.

name: NeoIPC BSI Hyperglycaemia
description: New and unexplained hyperglycaemia (> 140 mg/dl or > 7.8 mmol/L) or hypoglycaemia (<40 mg/dl or < 2.2 mmol/L).

name: NeoIPC BSI Interleukin
description: Increased level of interleukin (IL) 6 or 8.

name: NeoIPC BSI Irritability
formName: Irritability, lethargy, apathy or unstable condition
description: Irritability, lethargy, apathy or unstable condition.

name: NeoIPC BSI I/T-Ratio
description: I/T-Ratio (ratio of immature granulocytes to total granulocytes) > 0.2.

name: NeoIPC BSI Length of Stay
description: The Length of Stay on which the bloodstream infection occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

name: NeoIPC BSI No positive culture
description: No blood culture taken or negative blood culture.

name: NeoIPC BSI Perfusion
description: Capillary refill time (CRF) of > 3s, skin mottling or core/peripheral temperature gap > 2 °C

name: NeoIPC BSI Platelet count
description: Platelet count < 100 ×10⁹/L (100 ×10³/µL)

name: NeoIPC BSI Procalcitonin
description: Procalcitonin ≥ 2μg/L (2 ng/mL; 200 ng/dL)

name: NeoIPC BSI Temperature
description: Fever (> 38 °C) or hypothermia (< 36.5 °C) or temperature instability.

name: NeoIPC BSI White blood cell count
description: White blood cell count > 4 ×10⁹/L (4000/µL)

name: NeoIPC HAP Bradycardias
description: New/more frequent episodes of bradycardia with a heart rate of less than 80 beats per minute or otherwise unexplained tachycardia with a resting heart rate of more than 200 beats per minute.

name: NeoIPC HAP Device association
description: A Pneumonia is  is considered as device-associated if the patient had invasive ventilation or non-invasive ventilatory support for at least 3 consecutive days on the day of infection (=first symptoms or first positive culture) or the day before. Ventilaton via endotracheal or tracheostomy tube is considered as invasive ventilation. CPAP or High-Flow Nasal Cannula is considered as non-invasive ventilation. Note: If both invasive and non-invasive ventilation were used intermittently, a pneumonia should be recorded as INV-associated pneumonia.

name: NeoIPC HAP Day of life
description: The day of life on which the pneumonia occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

name: NeoIPC HAP Fever
description: Fever (> 38 °C) or hypothermia (< 36.5 °C) or temperature instability.

name: NeoIPC HAP Imaging findings
description: At least one of the following imaging findings (imaging technologies: X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound) shows new changes suggestive of pneumonia, such as: infiltrate, shadowing, opacification, increased density, fluid in the intrapleural cavity or interlobar fissure.

name: NeoIPC HAP Increased respiratory secretion
description: More frequent endotrachel suctioning required.

name: NeoIPC HAP Laboratory findings
description: There is at least one laboratory test result with a C-reactive protein (CRP) value of more than 20 mg/L (2 mg/dL) or an increased level of interleukin (IL) 6 or 8

name: NeoIPC HAP Length of Stay
description: The Length of Stay on which the pneumonia occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

name: NeoIPC HAP Microbiological test result
description: Availability of test results from a culture- or non-culture based microbiologic test from the respiratory tract specimen.

name: NeoIPC HAP Purulent tracheal aspirate
description: Purulent tracheal aspirate

name: NeoIPC HAP Respiratory distress
description: New or more frequent symptoms of respiratory distress (retractions, nasal flaring, grunting).

name: NeoIPC HAP Respiratory support
formName: Beginning or increase in respiratory support
description: New initiation of respiratory support or escalation of existing level of respiratory support* for ≥ 2 days after at least two days of stability.

name: NeoIPC HAP Secondary bloodstream infection
description: A BSI that is thought to be seeded from this hospital-acquired pneumonia. A BSI can be attributed to a site-specific infection (in this case the hospital-acquired pneumonia) if it occurs in a 17-day period that includes the day of infection (=first symptoms of site-specific infection), 3 days prior, and 13 days after.

name: NeoIPC HAP Tachypnoea
description: New or increased frequency of episodes of apnea lasting more than 20 seconds, or new or more frequent tachypnoea (>60/min).

name: NeoIPC NEC Abdominal distension
description: Distended abdomen.

name: NeoIPC NEC Abdominal discoloration
description: Abdominal discoloration or shiny/reddish skin tone.

name: NeoIPC NEC Bilious gastric aspirate
description: Bilious gastric aspirate (not from transpyloric feeding tube).

name: NeoIPC NEC Blood in stool
formName: Occult or visible blood in stool
description: Repeated occult (guaiac test) or visible blood in stool (no anal fissure).

name: NeoIPC NEC Extensive bowel necrosis
description: Surgical or pathologic finding of extensive bowel necrosis (>2 cm bowel affected).

name: NeoIPC NEC Day of life
description: The day of life on which the NEC occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

name: NeoIPC NEC Fixed bowel loops
description: Fixed bowel loops (>= 24h).

name: NeoIPC NEC Increased gastric residuals
description: Increased gastric residuals from previous feeding.

name: NeoIPC NEC Length of Stay
description: The Length of Stay on which the NEC occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

name: NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis imaging
description: Pneumatosis intestinalis in imaging findings (imaging technologies: X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound).

name: NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis surgical
formName: Pneumatosis intestinalis with or without intestinal perforation
description: Surgical or pathologic finding of pneumatosis intestinalis with or without intestinal perforation.

name: NeoIPC NEC Pneumoperitoneum
description: Pneumoperitoneum in imaging findings (imaging technologies: X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound).

name: NeoIPC NEC Portal venous gas
description: Accumulation of gas(-bubbles) in the portal vein and its branches.

name: NeoIPC NEC Secondary bloodstream infection
description: A BSI that is thought to be seeded from this NEC A BSI can be attributed to a site-specific infection (in this case the NEC if it occurs in a 17-day period that includes the day of infection (=first symptoms of site-specific infection), 3 days prior, and 13 days after.

name: NeoIPC NEC Vomiting
description: Increasing/pronounced vomiting (e.g. bilious or bloody).

name: NeoIPC SSI Abscess deep incision
formName: Abscess or other evidence of infection involving the deep incision
description: An abscess or other evidence of infection involving the deep incision that is detected on gross anatomical or histopathologic exam, or imaging test.

name: NeoIPC SSI Abscess organ space
formName: Abscess or other evidence of infection involving the organ/space
description: An abscess or other evidence of infection involving the organ/space that is detected on gross anatomical or histopathologic exam, or imaging test evidence suggestive of infection.

name: NeoIPC SSI Day of life
description: The day of life on which the surgical site infection occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

name: NeoIPC SSI Fever
description: Fever (> 38 °C) or hypothermia (< 36.5 °C) or temperature instability.

name: NeoIPC SSI Deep incision dehisces
formName: Deep incision dehisces, opened or aspirated
description: A deep incision that spontaneously dehisces, or is deliberately opened or aspirated by a surgeon, physician or physician designee.

name: NeoIPC SSI Superficial incision opened
description: Superficial incision is deliberately opened by a surgeon, physician or physician designee

name: NeoIPC SSI Infection present
formName: Infection present at time of surgery
description: Select "Yes" only if the evidence of infection seen (seen) during the surgical procedure applies to the depth of the SSI attributed to the procedure. For example, if a patient has a documented intra-abdominal infection at the time of surgery and later returns with an organ/space infection, Set "Infection present at the time of surgery" to "Yes". If a patient has a documented intra-abdominal infection at the time of surgery and later returns with superficial or deep incisional infection, select "No" for "Infection present at time of surgery".

name: NeoIPC SSI Infection type
description: A superficial incisional SSI involves only skin and subcutaneous tissue of the incision. A deep incisional SSI involves deep soft tissues of the incision (for example, fascial and muscle layers). An organ/space SSI involves any part of the body deeper than the fascial/muscle layers that is opened or manipulated during the operative procedure.

name: NeoIPC SSI Localized erythema
description: A localized erythema in the area of the incision.

name: NeoIPC SSI Localized heat
description: Localized heat in the area of the incision.

name: NeoIPC SSI Localized pain deep
description: Localized pain or tenderness in the area of the incision.

name: NeoIPC SSI Localized pain superficial
description: Localized pain or tenderness in the area of the incision.

name: NeoIPC SSI Localized swelling
description: Localized swelling in the area of the incision.

name: NeoIPC SSI length of stay
description: The length of stay  on which the surgical site infection occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

name: NeoIPC SSI Organisms identified from deep tissue of incision
shortName: NeoIPC SSI Organisms from deep tissue
formName: Organism(s) identified from deep tissue of incision
description: At least one organism has been identified from the deep tissue of the incision by a culture or non-culture based microbiologic testing method which is performed for purposes of clinical diagnosis or treatment (for example, NOT Active Surveillance Culture/Testing (ASC/AST)).

name: NeoIPC SSI Organisms identified from organ space fluid tissue
shortName: NeoIPC SSI Organisms from organ space fluid tissue
formName: Organism(s) identified from organ/space fluid or tissue
description: At least one organism has been identified from fluid or tissue in the organ/space by a culture or non-culture based microbiologic testing method which is performed for purposes of clinical diagnosis or treatment (for example, NOT Active Surveillance Culture/Testing (ASC/AST)).

name: NeoIPC SSI Organisms identified from superficial incision
shortName: NeoIPC SSI Organisms from superficial incision
description: At least one organism has been identified from an aseptically obtained specimen from the superficial incision or subcutaneous tissue by a culture or non-culture based microbiologic testing method which is performed for purposes of clinical diagnosis or treatment (for example, NOT Active Surveillance Culture/Testing (ASC/AST)).

name: NeoIPC SSI Diagnosis superficial SSI by physician
shortName: NeoIPC SSI Diagnosis superf. SSI by physician
description: Diagnosis of a superficial incisional SSI by a surgeon, physician or physician designee.

name: NeoIPC SSI Purulent drainage deep incision
shortName: NeoIPC SSI Purulent drain. deep inc.
formName: Purulent drainage from the deep incision
description: Purulent drainage from deep layers of the incision.

name: NeoIPC SSI Purulent drainage drain
description: Purulent drainage from a drain placed into the organ/space (for example, closed suction drainage system, open drain, T-tube drain, CT guided drainage)

name: NeoIPC SSI Purulent drainage superficial incision
shortName: NeoIPC SSI Purulent drain. superf. inc.
formName: Purulent drainage from the superficial incision
description: Purulent drainage from the superficial layer of the incision.

name: NeoIPC SSI Secondary bloodstream infection
description: A BSI that is thought to be seeded from the SSI A BSI can be attributed to a site-specific infection (in this case the SSI if it occurs in a 17-day period that includes the day of infection (=first symptoms of site-specific infection), 3 days prior, and 13 days after.

name: NeoIPC Surgery ASA-Score
description: The American Society of Anesthesiologists (ASA) Physical Status Classification System to assess and communicate a patient’s pre-anesthesia medical co-morbidities.

name: NeoIPC SURGERY Day of life
description: The day of life on which the surgery occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

name: NeoIPC Surgery Duration
description: The duration of the surgical procedure in minutes (incision-to-suture time if available).

name: NeoIPC Surgery Emergency procedure
description: Yes: A procedure that is documented per the facility’s protocol to be an emergency or urgent procedure. No: The intervention is initiated and performed in a planned manner Unknown: No information available.

name: NeoIPC Surgery Endoscopic procedure
description: Select "Yes" if the procedure was performed completely endoscopically and "No" if the procedure was performed open or endoscopically assisted, or when switching to an open technique during an endoscopic procedure occurred.

name: NeoIPC Surgery Implant
description: An implant is a foreign body of non-human origin that is permanently placed into a patient during an operation and is not routinely manipulated for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes (e.g.; vascular prostheses, screws, wires, meshes).

name: NeoIPC Surgery Infection signs
formName: Signs of infection at time of surgery
description: Complete this field if signs of infection were identified during the surgical procedure. The signs of infection must be noted intraoperatively and documented in the narrative part of the operation note or report of surgery. If a surgical site infection occurs, this information will be used to determine if the signs listed in this section can be interpreted as "Infection present at time of surgery".

name: NeoIPC SURGERY Length of Stay
description: The Length of Stay on which the surgery occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

name: NeoIPC Surgery Main procedure code
description: The International Classification of Health Interventions (ICHI) code of the main procedure performed. If multiple different procedures are performed during one surgery, the surgeon decides which one is the main procedure (typically the most complex procedure or the on causing the highest risk for infection).

name: NeoIPC Surgery Primary closure
description: The closure of the skin level during the original surgery, regardless of the presence of wires, wicks, drains, or other devices or objects extruding through the incision. This category includes surgeries where the skin is closed by some means. Thus, if any portion of the incision is closed at the skin level, by any manner, a designation of primary closure should be assigned to the surgery. Note: If a procedure has multiple incision/laparoscopic trocar sites and any of the incisions are closed primarily then the procedure technique is recorded as primary closed.

name: NeoIPC Surgery Procedure description
description: A human-readable name or description of the surgical procedure as it it typically called by surgeons in your institution (e.g.; ligation of patent arterial duct).

name: NeoIPC Surgery Revision procedure
description: Revision procedures are follow-up, replacement or corrective procedures after an initial procedure. A revision procedure terminates the follow-up for the primary procedure and starts a new follwo-up period.

name: NeoIPC Surgery Side procedure code 1
description: The International Classification of Health Interventions (ICHI) code of a side procedure performed. If multiple different procedures are performed during one surgery, the surgeon decides which one is the main procedure (typically the most complex procedure or the on causing the highest risk for infection) and which ones are side procedures.

name: NeoIPC Surgery Side procedure code 2
description: The International Classification of Health Interventions (ICHI) code of a side procedure performed. If multiple different procedures are performed during one surgery, the surgeon decides which one is the main procedure (typically the most complex procedure or the on causing the highest risk for infection) and which ones are side procedures.

name: NeoIPC Surgery Wound class
description: An assessment of the degree of contamination of a surgical wound at the time of the surgical procedure. Wound class is assigned by a person involved in the surgical procedure (for example, surgeon, circulating nurse, etc.). The four wound classifications are: Clean, Clean-Contaminated, Contaminated, and Infected.

name: NeoIPC Surveillance end AB days
description: The cumulative number of days when the infant received (any) systemic antibiotics. Only one antibiotic day can be recorded per day, which mans that a day when the patient received multiple antibiotics is still counted as one antibiotic day.

name: NeoIPC Surveillance end CVC days
description: The cumulative number of days when a central venous catheter was in place for at least 12 hours/day.

name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Human milk days
description: The cumulative number of days the patient’s enteral feeding exclusively consists of (own mother’s or donor) breast milk. Fortified breast milk is considered as breast milk.

name: NeoIPC Surveillance end INV days
description: The cumulative number of days when the infant was on invasive ventilation (intubated) for at least 12 hours/day.

name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Kangaroo care days
description: The cumulative number of days the patient received kangaroo care (intensive skin-to-skin-contact) for at least 2 hours.

name: NeoIPC Surveillance end NIV days
description: The cumulative number of days when the infant was on non-invasive ventilation (not intubated; e.g. high flow nasal cannulae) for at least 12 hours/day.

name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days
description: The number of days the patient stayed in the department during the current admission (enrollment). This field is calculated automatically.

name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Probiotic days
description: The cumulative number of days the patient receives an oral probiotic containing at least one of Lactobacillus spp. or Bifidobacterium spp., regardless of the amount.

name: NeoIPC Surveillance end PVC days
description: The cumulative number of days when a peripheral venous catheter was in place for at least 12 hours/day.

name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Reason
description: Reason for the end of surveillance. This can be either death or the discharge/transfer of the patient.
